slug: Shubh Swastik Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
name: Shubh Swastik Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
shortDescription: A handcrafted pendant necklace featuring the sacred Saraswati Chowki Aipan motif.
description: Drawn by hand, one line at a time, this Aipan pendant necklace carries a tradition passed from Kumaoni mothers to daughters for generations. The Saraswati Chowki motif represents the goddess of knowledge, wisdom, and arts.
motifMeaning: Saraswati Aipan is a sacred geometric pattern dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, symbolizing knowledge, music, and creative arts. It is traditionally drawn during educational ceremonies and Vasant Panchami.
materials: Brass base with Geru red and rice white enamel, cotton cord
careInstructions: Wipe gently with a soft dry cloth. Keep away from water and perfumes.

slug: Divine Mata Rani Hand-Drawn Folk Art Frame
name: Divine Mata Rani Hand-Drawn Folk Art Frame
shortDescription: A magnificent wall hanging depicting the nine forms of Goddess Durga in traditional Aipan style.
description: This intricate wall hanging features the Nav Durga Chowki pattern, representing the nine divine forms of Goddess Durga. Each geometric segment tells a story of feminine power and protection.
motifMeaning: Nav Durga Chowki depicts the nine manifestations of Goddess Durga. Each geometric form represents a different aspect of the divine feminine — protection, strength, nurturing, wisdom, courage, peace, prosperity, creativity, and liberation.
materials: Handwoven cotton canvas, natural Geru, rice paste
careInstructions: Keep in dry place away from direct sunlight. Dust gently with a soft brush.

slug: Shree Ganesh Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
name: Shree Ganesh Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
shortDescription: A spacious tote bag with a hand-painted lotus mandala motif.
description: Carry a piece of Kumaon with you. This hand-painted tote bag features a beautiful lotus mandala, a motif that symbolizes purity, spiritual awakening, and the beauty of the mountains.
motifMeaning: The art represents purity and spiritual awakening. In Aipan tradition, it is drawn to invite positive energy and divine grace into the home.
materials: 100% organic cotton canvas, Geru and rice paint
careInstructions: Hand wash cold. Dry in shade. Do not bleach or wring.

slug: Sacred Trishul Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
name: Sacred Trishul Mandala Aipan Cloth Art
shortDescription: A set of 6 hand-painted coasters featuring Diya and Swastika motifs.
description: These coasters bring the warmth of Kumaoni tradition to your everyday living. Each coaster is hand-painted with traditional Diya and Swastika patterns, symbols of prosperity and auspiciousness.
motifMeaning: The Diya (lamp) symbolizes light overcoming darkness, while the Swastika represents auspiciousness and good fortune. Together they invoke blessings, prosperity, and positive energy.
materials: Natural stone, Geru and rice paint, felt backing
careInstructions: Wipe with a damp cloth. Avoid soaking in water.

name: Limited Edition Ritual Thali Plate
shortDescription: A premium hand-painted ritual thali for sacred ceremonies.
description: This limited edition ritual thali plate is hand-painted with sacred Aipan motifs. Used traditionally during poojas and ceremonies, each thali is a unique piece of functional art that carries the blessings of the mountains.
motifMeaning: Lakshmi Chowki is dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the deity of wealth and prosperity. This pattern is traditionally drawn during Diwali and housewarming ceremonies to invoke abundance.
materials: Brass, natural Geru and rice paste coat
careInstructions: Hand wash gently. Dry immediately. Do not use in microwave.

slug: Shree Ganesh Aipan Karwa Chauth & Pooja Set
name: Shree Ganesh Aipan Karwa Chauth & Pooja Set
shortDescription: A set of 4 bookmarks with miniature Aipan mountain motifs.
description: These bookmarks capture the essence of the Kumaon Himalayas. Each bookmark features a unique Aipan motif representing a different mountain blessing — peace, strength, wisdom, and creativity.
motifMeaning: The mountain motifs in Aipan represent the Himalayas, considered sacred as the abode of gods. Each pattern invokes the blessings of the mountains — protection, stability, wisdom, and spiritual elevation.
materials: Handmade paper, Geru and rice paint, silk tassel
careInstructions: Keep away from moisture. Handle with care.

name: Traditional Swastik Aipan Kalash
shortDescription: A sacred wooden platform hand-painted with traditional Aipan patterns.
description: This traditional Pooja Chowki is a sacred platform used for daily worship and ceremonies. Hand-carved from mango wood and painted with authentic Aipan patterns using natural Geru and rice paste. Each chowki is blessed before it leaves the workshop.
motifMeaning: Sarvatobhadra means 'auspicious from all directions.' This comprehensive Aipan pattern includes multiple protective symbols arranged to bring blessings from all directions.
materials: Mango wood, natural Geru, rice paste, saffron
careInstructions: Wipe with a dry cloth. Keep in a dry place. Avoid water.

slug: Shree Om Yantra Aipan Wall Frame
name: Shree Om Yantra Aipan Wall Frame
shortDescription: A magnificent wall art piece featuring the auspicious Lakshmi Chowki pattern.
description: This wall art features the Lakshmi Chowki, the most revered Aipan pattern. Traditionally drawn on the floor of Kumaoni homes during festivals and ceremonies, this piece brings the sacred tradition to your wall.
motifMeaning: Lakshmi Chowki is dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the deity of wealth and prosperity. This pattern is traditionally drawn during Diwali and housewarming ceremonies to invoke abundance.
materials: Handwoven canvas, natural Geru, rice paste, wooden frame
careInstructions: Dust gently. Keep away from direct sunlight and moisture.
